I'm not saying you should but it there was ever a time to buy Bitcoin miners it's when literally everyone is bearish on the sector. Everyone hates BTC miners here but with a recovery in the asset you will probably also see a quick double or triple in names like BTDR and ABTC.

Currently on a first class flight to Dubai.

Noticed something interesting: economy passengers all looking out the windows and discussing the possibility of drone and missile attacks.

Every single person in first class is buying Bitcoin and coding with Claude.

Lesson in there.
Bitcoin doesn’t care about oil prices. The bear market is almost over.

I've spent 25 years building and improving operational systems for billion-dollar mining companies.

The kind of operations where a 2% efficiency gain is worth $50 million.

I'm still doing that work through Intifica and I love it.

But something happened at a BBQ that changed my trajectory. 👇

My good friend runs a trades business. 14 staff. Great reputation. Revenue is strong.

But he was drowning.

❌ Quoting took 3 hours per job
❌ Scheduling lived in his head
❌ Invoices went out weeks late
❌ Hadn't taken a proper holiday in 4 years (even him taking time off for the Sunday BBQ was a stretch!)

I started asking questions. Within 20 minutes I'd found the same bottlenecks I'd been solving at mining scale for two decades.

Identical problems. Identical root causes.

These are 𝗯𝘂𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗯𝗹𝗲𝗺𝘀. They've always been business problems. Bad processes, missing structure, too much relying on the owner's memory.

What's changed is that in the last 6 months, the 𝘁𝗼𝗼𝗹𝘀 𝗮𝘃𝗮𝗶𝗹𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘃𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗺 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝗮𝗱𝘃𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝗮𝘁 𝗮 𝗽𝗮𝗰𝗲 𝗺𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗽𝗲𝗼𝗽𝗹𝗲 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲𝗻'𝘁 𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗰𝗲𝗱.

Automation and AI have moved from "interesting experiment" to genuinely practical, but the 𝗮𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗴𝗮𝗽 𝗶𝘀 𝗺𝗮𝘀𝘀𝗶𝘃𝗲.

Small business owners don't have time to keep up. They're too busy running jobs, managing staff, and chasing invoices to figure out which tools could give them 10+ hours a week back.

I do have that time. I work with these tools every day. I test them, I break them, I know which ones deliver real value and which ones are noise.

Here's the gap that I couldn't unsee:

When BHP has these problems, they get a consulting team and a $200K engagement.

When a trades business owner has them? "Hustle harder."

There are 𝟰𝟰𝟲,𝟬𝟬𝟬 𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗱𝗲𝘀 𝗯𝘂𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝗻 𝗔𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗮. They keep the country running. And most of them are stuck operating on WhatsApp groups, paper notebooks, and the owner's memory.

So alongside Intifica, I've launched 𝗙𝗮𝗿𝗙𝗿𝗼𝗻𝘁.

Same systems thinking I've applied at billion-dollar scale but designed for trades businesses with 5–50 staff.

➡️ Fix the operations first
➡️ Then design and layer on the automation and AI that makes it stick
➡️ Business problem first, technology second

The goal: help trades business owners build operations that 𝗱𝗼𝗻'𝘁 𝗳𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝗮𝗽𝗮𝗿𝘁 𝘄𝗵𝗲𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘆 𝘁𝗮𝗸𝗲 𝗮 𝘄𝗲𝗲𝗸 𝗼𝗳𝗳.

—

🤝 If you know someone running a trades business who's working 55+ hours a week just to keep things moving, I'd genuinely appreciate an introduction. That's exactly who FarFront is built for.

And if that's you - let's have a conversation. No pitch, just a chat about what's eating your time.

🔗 https://t.co/V9e7PhZsEn, or DM me.

What just happened to Iran's Kharg Island?

President Trump just said the US has carried out the "most powerful bombing raids in Middle East history" on Kharg island.

This is a MAJOR escalation for oil markets. Here's why:

Kharg Island has been described as the "crown jewel" of Iran's oil industry. It is a vital, tiny island in the northern Persian Gulf that manages ~90% of Iran's crude oil exports.

Kharg Island alone handles ~2% of global oil supply.

In the lead up to the war, Iran was exporting as much as 3 MILLION barrels of oil per day from Kharg Island. Trump said that the US military has "chosen NOT to wipe out the Oil Infrastructure on the Island" for now.

However, President Trump also said he will reconsider this decision should Iran "do anything to interfere with the free and safe passage of Ships through the Strait of Hormuz," which Iran is clearly doing right now.

In other words, President Trump appears to be paving the path for the destruction of oil infrastructure on one of the world's most crucial oil ports and islands.

It's no coincidence this came just 2 hours after markets closed for the weekend.

Buckle up for a busy weekend ahead.

BREAKING: President Trump says the US just carried out "one of the most powerful bombing raids in the history of the Middle East" targeting Iran's Kharg Island.

Trump also threatens to "wipe out" Iran's oil infrastructure on the island if Iran interferes with the "free and safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz."
BREAKING: The Trump Administration will receive a $10 billion fee for brokering the sale of TikTok, per WSJ.

US farms are disappearing at an alarming rate:

The number of US farms fell -15,000 YoY in 2025, to 1.87 million, the lowest since the 1850s.

This marks the 3rd annual decline and the 6th over the last 7 years.

Over this period, the number of farms has dropped -158,200, or -8%.

At the same time, total farmland has fallen -24.9 million acres, to 874 million, the lowest since the 1910s.

Meanwhile, farm bankruptcy filings surged +36% YoY, to 293 in the first 9 months of 2025.

US farms are in a long-term decline.

BREAKING: President Trump has rejected an offer from Russia's President Putin to move Iran's enriched uranium to Russia in a deal to end the war, per Axios.

Details include:

1. In a phone call with Trump, Putin proposed moving Iran's enriched uranium to Russia as part of a deal to end the war

2. Securing Iran's 450 kilograms of 60%-enriched uranium remains a top priority for the US

3. Trump has acknowledged for the first time that Russia is aiding Iran in the war

4. The US position is we need to see the uranium secured," a US official said

BREAKING: A record 104.3 million Americans are now "outside" of the labor force.

These are individuals who are neither employed nor actively looking for work, including retirees, students, stay-at-home parents, and discouraged workers.

This exceeds the 2020 pandemic peak by 600,000 when nearly the entire economy was shut down.

Since the 2008 Financial Crisis, the number of people not in the labor force has surged +25.7 million and +35.1 million since 2000.

As a % of the labor force, this metric stands at 61.2%, the highest level since the 1970s, excluding the pandemic.

All while the US population stands at a record 342.5 million, up +61.4 million since January 2000.

Cracks in the labor market are spreading.

Trump said the US could “take apart Iran’s electric capacity within one hour” but hasn’t done it yet.

His words: “If they do that, the whole region will go dark in less than half an hour and darkness provides ample opportunity to hunt down US servicemen running for safety.”

He’s not just threatening to retaliate. He’s saying if you kill our power grid, we kill every power grid in the Gulf. And then we go after your soldiers in the dark.

This is the same guy who last week told Trump to “be careful not to be eliminated.”

Here’s why this threat is credible.

– Iran has already hit power infrastructure in the UAE

– Already struck a power station in Fujairah supporting desalination

– Already damaged Kuwait’s airport fuel systems

– Already struck Saudi Aramco’s biggest refinery
